headline: Space names crater on SpaceX IPO overhang; LatAm holds better
conviction_update: MELI -1.5% underperforms modestly amid mixed sell-side sentiment; bullish long-term coverage dominates but near-term momentum soft post-earnings miss. NU -1.9% drifts lower in thin flow — no company-specific catalyst, thesis intact as digital banking penetration in Brazil/Mexico continues unabated. Both names trading at compelling discounts to targets (49%/31% upside) with no fundamental deterioration evident.

watchlist_movers.note: Down 6.8% as space basket unwinds post-SpaceX IPO euphoria; satellite connectivity thesis unchanged but volatility elevated.
Matching ASTS decline despite naval contract tailwinds elsewhere in sector; SpaceX IPO narrative creating near-term headwind.
UBS cuts PT to $60 maintaining Neutral; Microsoft-Nvidia nuclear angle provides narrative support but valuation reset underway.

market_context: Broad risk-off session across EM tech and frontier names as SpaceX IPO filing redirects capital flows and raises competitive benchmarking concerns.
